checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=82063574.94 (2013-09-30) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=88652530.74 (Q3 2013) implying DSO ~84 days, rising to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=111474930.42 (2013-12-31) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=94705406.51 (Q4 2013) implying DSO ~107 days
evidence.location: 10-Q comparative balance sheet and income statement data

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No Inventory concept present in provided series; company reports CostOfServices and SalesRevenueServicesNet, consistent with a services-based business model
Gross margin computed from Revenues=287715260.21 and CostOfServices=92982026.87 (2011 annual, ~67.7%) vs Revenues=355149917.14 and CostOfServices=111379599.79 (2013 annual, ~68.6%) — stable

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=18938605.18 (2010-12-31) growing to 41440347.30 (2013-12-31), tracking business growth

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q filed 2013-05-03 (accession not separately listed) followed by 10-Q/A filed 2013-05-13, only 10 days later
Repeated pattern of 10-K/A filings shortly after each annual 10-K: 2012-04-27, 2013-04-29, 2014-04-29 (following 10-Ks filed 2012-02-22, 2013-02-20, 2014-02-18)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Series spans annual and quarterly data from 2009 through Q3 2014 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, cash flow statement items, and balance sheet items across multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition or extension of customer credit terms (e.g., bill-and-hold, channel stuffing, or contract modifications at period end) causing receivables to build up disproportionately relative to reported revenue, concentrated at fiscal year-end 2013 and persisting into H1 2014.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=84618930.97 (2012-12-31) growing to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=111474930.42 (2013-12-31), a 31.7% increase vs Revenues growth of 12.4% (Revenues=315944268.30 in 2012 to Revenues=355149917.14 in 2013)

Quarterly DSO implied by AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=82063574.94 (2013-09-30) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=88652530.74 (Q3 2013, ~84 days) rising to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=111474930.42 (2013-12-31) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=94705406.51 (Q4 2013, ~107 days)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Large and persistent gap between negative net income and strongly positive operating cash flow could reflect heavy reliance on non-cash addbacks (depreciation/amortization, stock-based compensation) and cash collected under deferred revenue arrangements, potentially masking underlying operating losses and creating incentive to manage working-capital or revenue timing to sustain cash flow optics.

Rapid post-filing correction (10-Q/A) filed only 10 days after the original Q1 2013 10-Q suggests a data or classification error was identified and corrected quickly, which could indicate weaker internal controls around period-end account balances such as receivables/payables that were later revised.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=4073217.69 (2013-03-31) reported in 10-Q/A filed 2013-05-13, following an original 10-Q filed 2013-05-03

top_signals: Receivables grew 31.7% in FY2013 vs revenue growth of only 12.4%, with implied DSO expanding from ~84 days (Q3 2013) to ~107 days (Q4 2013) and remaining elevated through H1 2014
Persistent divergence between negative net income and strongly positive operating cash flow across 2011-2013 (e.g., NetIncomeLoss=-14595490.39 vs CFO=55554232.32 in 2012)
Rapid 10-Q/A amendment filed only 10 days after the original Q1 2013 10-Q, indicating a quickly-identified correction
Allowance for doubtful accounts and accrued liabilities grew in line with or faster than receivables/business activity, and gross margins were stable, reducing concern on those specific dimensions
Earnings were volatile quarter-to-quarter (not artificially smoothed), which is inconsistent with typical earnings-management smoothing patterns
